scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All pass criteria met: throughput gap clearly identified (300/min vs 15-20/min), unbounded queue growth described, visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with arithmetic (10*3s=30s >= 30s timeout), missing DLQ identified as a blocker, queue depth metric gap called out as critical, autoscaling recommended (15-20 instances), verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput math is precise and framed as a 15-20x deficit, not just a vague 'mismatch'
Visibility timeout risk explained with clear arithmetic showing it triggers under normal conditions, not just edge cases
DLQ absence correctly framed as compounding the capacity problem, not just a best practice
Queue depth metric gap called out as CRITICAL with clear operational impact — on-call cannot distinguish healthy from 30-min-behind
Goes beyond rubric requirements with additional valuable findings: circuit breaker absence, memory exhaustion risk, idempotency gap, rollback concerns
Confidence section is well-calibrated with honest assumptions and low-confidence areas
